info.title: Insurance Pricing API
info.summary: HTTP inference API for the insurance premium pricing model.
info.description:
Production-style FastAPI surface for serving insurance pricing predictions.

## Scope
- score raw insurance business records
- expose frequency, severity, and final premium predictions
- keep the served model bundle pinned at startup through `INSURANCE_PRICING_RUN_ID`

## Request Contract
Send the raw columns from the historical scoring dataset, such as the ones present in `data/test.csv`. Feature engineering stays inside the service layer, so clients only need to send business-facing fields.

## Response Headers
Every response includes `X-Request-ID` and `X-Process-Time-Ms` headers to help trace calls across clients, logs, and deployment environments.

## Batch Behavior
Batch endpoints preserve input order so clients can safely align each prediction with the submitted records.

## Product Quote Endpoints
The public `/quotes` endpoints persist end-user quote history scoped to an anonymous `X-Client-ID` header so the Next.js frontend can retrieve prior simulations.

get.description: Quick navigation endpoint exposing the main operational and documentation URLs of the current API instance.

200.description: Top-level navigation links for the current API instance.

get.description: Returns the installed package version together with the model run identifier configured at application startup.

200.description: Package version and currently served model run identifier.

get.description: Returns the metadata of the model bundle currently loaded in memory, including artifacts, metrics, feature schema, and training configuration summary.

200.description: Metadata describing the model bundle currently served by this API instance.

get.description: Confirms that the API process is running and that the configured model bundle was loaded during startup.

200.description: Operational status of the API process and the loaded model bundle.

get.description: Confirms that the API has completed startup and is ready to accept inference traffic from clients, reverse proxies, or orchestrators.

200.description: Readiness status of the API instance.

get.description: Returns a client-friendly summary of the raw fields expected by the prediction endpoints, including required vs optional fields. Use this endpoint before integrating a client, a frontend form, or a batch scoring job.

200.description: Summary of the single-record and batch input contract for prediction endpoints.

post.description:
Scores one insurance record and returns only the calibrated frequency component.

This endpoint is useful when you want to inspect the claim occurrence probability without combining it with the severity model.

schema.description: Raw business-facing fields for one insurance contract.

200.description: Frequency prediction for a single insurance record.

422.description: Payload validation failed. The request must match the raw business fields expected by the model input contract.

500.description: The model bundle could not score the request. Check the payload semantics and the loaded artifact integrity.

503.description: Prediction persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

post.description: Scores multiple insurance records and returns frequency predictions in the same order as the request payload.

schema.description: Batch scoring payload for frequency inference. Input order is preserved in the response, which makes the endpoint suitable for bulk scoring pipelines.

200.description: Frequency predictions for multiple insurance records.

422.description: Payload validation failed. The request must match the raw business fields expected by the model input contract.

500.description: The model bundle could not score the request. Check the payload semantics and the loaded artifact integrity.

503.description: Prediction persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

post.description:
Scores one insurance record and returns only the severity component.

Use this endpoint when you want to inspect the conditional expected claim cost without the frequency layer.

schema.description: Raw business-facing fields for one insurance contract. The payload structure is the same as for the other prediction endpoints.

200.description: Severity prediction for a single insurance record.

422.description: Payload validation failed. The request must match the raw business fields expected by the model input contract.

500.description: The model bundle could not score the request. Check the payload semantics and the loaded artifact integrity.

503.description: Prediction persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

post.description: Scores multiple insurance records and returns severity predictions in the same order as the request payload.

schema.description: Batch scoring payload for severity inference. Each input record produces exactly one output prediction in the returned `predictions` list.

200.description: Severity predictions for multiple insurance records.

422.description: Payload validation failed. The request must match the raw business fields expected by the model input contract.

500.description: The model bundle could not score the request. Check the payload semantics and the loaded artifact integrity.

503.description: Prediction persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

post.description:
Scores one insurance record and returns the full pricing decomposition: frequency, severity, and final premium.

This is the main endpoint to call when you want the final commercial premium output.

schema.description: Raw business-facing fields for one insurance contract. The server computes engineered features internally before running the model bundle.

200.description: Frequency, severity, and premium predictions for a single insurance record.

422.description: Payload validation failed. The request must match the raw business fields expected by the model input contract.

500.description: The model bundle could not score the request. Check the payload semantics and the loaded artifact integrity.

503.description: Prediction persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

post.description: Scores multiple insurance records and returns the full pricing decomposition for each one in the same order as the input payload.

schema.description: Batch scoring payload for full premium inference. This endpoint is designed for bulk scoring while preserving the original order of the submitted contracts.

200.description: Frequency, severity, and premium predictions for multiple insurance records.

422.description: Payload validation failed. The request must match the raw business fields expected by the model input contract.

500.description: The model bundle could not score the request. Check the payload semantics and the loaded artifact integrity.

503.description: Prediction persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

409.description: A user already exists with this email.

200.description: Successful Response
Successful Response

post.description: Scores one insurance record, persists the input payload and the resulting quote, and returns the stored quote detail for the current customer context.

schema.description: Raw business-facing fields for one insurance contract. The payload mirrors the technical prediction contract used by the model.

200.description: Persisted quote detail for the current customer context.

400.description: The required X-Client-ID header is missing or invalid.

503.description: Quote persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

get.description: Returns the quote history associated with the current browser context or authenticated account, ordered from the most recent to the oldest quote.

200.description: Persisted quote history for the current customer context.

400.description: The required X-Client-ID header is missing or invalid.

503.description: Quote persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

get.description: Returns one persisted quote when it belongs to the current browser context, the current account, or when it is accessed by an administrator.

200.description: Persisted quote detail for the current customer context.

400.description: The required X-Client-ID header is missing or invalid.

404.description: The quote does not exist for the current customer context.

503.description: Quote persistence is unavailable because PostgreSQL is not ready.

get.summary: Generate the PDF report for one quote

200.description: Successful Response
Successful Response
Successful Response
